title: Lindernia bonatii
taxonomy: Lindernia bonatii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Linderniaceae. It belongs to the genus Lindernia, which consists of approximately 150 species of aquatic or semi-aquatic plants. The plant is native to Asia and is often found in wet environments, such as riverbanks and lakeshores. Lindernia bonatii is a small, delicate species that produces small white or pink flowers. As a member of the Linderniaceae family, Lindernia bonatii is closely related to other species of flowering plants that share similar characteristics.

intro: Lindernia bonatii is a species of flowering plant in the family Linderniaceae, commonly known as the false pimpernel. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage.
